title: GIFT-seq: Nuclei Preparation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"b7tld","text":"This protocol can be used for preparation of Formalin-Fixed Paraffin-Embedded (FFPE) Tissue Derived Nuclei. Together should be used - 10x Genomics \"Fixation of Cells \u0026 Nuclei for GEM-X Flex Gene Expression\" (CG000782 | Rev D). This protocol should be followed by \"GIFT-seq: Gapfill and Library Preparation Protocol\".","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":134,"length":91},{"style":"bold","offset":263,"length":53}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"391r8","text":"Maintain an RNAse free environment when preparing buffers and throughout the protocol by spraying bench with RNaseZap™ and using molecular grade reagents when possible.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"30h2s","text":"To increase cell recovery, opt for a swinging bucket rotor when pelleting and leave a 10-30 µL of liquid behind when aspirating buffers.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eb42e","text":"When possible, use low-binding plasticware.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"etgcr","text":"Use formaldehyde with caution as it is a hazardous material.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"7ef9","text":"Sorting \u0026 Collection (S\u0026C) Buffer: 1x PBS (Corning cat# 21-040-CV) + 0.02% BSA (Gibco cat# 15260037) + 0.2 U/uL RNase Inhibitor (Sigma Aldrich cat# 3335402001)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":42,"length":24},{"style":"italic","offset":79,"length":21},{"style":"italic","offset":128,"length":31}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a6lud","text":"Long-term Storage Buffer: Water (Invitrogen cat# 10977-015) + 1x Quenching Buffer B (10x Genomics PN-2001300) + 10% Glycerol (Millipore Sigma cat# G5516-100ML) + 0.1 Enhancer (10x Genomics PN-2000482) (warmed for 10 minutes at 65°C 1000 rpm)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":32,"length":27},{"style":"italic","offset":84,"length":25},{"style":"italic","offset":125,"length":34},{"style":"italic","offset":175,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aggsf","text":"Additive in ~20 mL RNAse/DNAse free Water + 4.08 mL 10x PBS (Corning cat# 46-013-CM)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":82}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"99pa7","text":"Progressively add 1 N NaOH (Millipore Sigma cat# S2770-100ML) to dissolve, should start dissolving around 5.5 mL 1 N NaOH and slowly add more. Check pH with pH paper once all powder is dissolved, and add NaOH until pH ~7.4. May have to vortex and moderately heat to 37°C.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":271}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4arj4","text":"Top up to 40.8 mL with water. Filter, aliquot for single-use 500 µL and freeze. Not tested for freeze and thaw cycles.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":118}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: NUCLEI ISOLATION

Obtain 2 to 6 FFPE curls each 50 µm to 80 µm of thickness. Curls can be stored in a 1.5mL tube at 4 °C for long term storage. Number of curls and thickness will change based on how much tissue is embedded and what is desired or needed number of nuclei.

Preparation: For each sample, pre-cool 2 NIC+ (yellow) cartridges (S2 Genomics) at 4 °C or On ice . Turn on Singulator and tablet. Start FFPE protocol and let machine preheat 00:10:00, approx. . Singulator 100 System (Tissue dissociation, S2 Genomics, 100-067-764) Note. If pop-up appears saying that temperature isn't reached, press "Wait".

During the run, pre-cool Singulator 200 for subsequent nuclei extraction from rehydrated tissue. Upon completion of the protocol, remove the cartridge from the machine. Singulator 200 System (Tissue dissociation, S2 Genomics)
Once S100 reaches temperature, insert NIC+ Cartridge with flattened out curl. Start run. Deparaffination and rehydration takes 00:28:00 .

Take 1 NIC+ Cartridge, remove grinder from the cartridge and add curls to the compartment. Put grinder head back. Manually press and spin grinder head a few times to make sure curls are flat on the bottom of the cartridge chamber.

Transfer tissue to cold NIC+ cartridge one of the following ways: Transfer tissue pieces using forceps to NIC+ cartridge for nuclei isolation and add 500 µL of nuclei isolation reagent (NIR; S2 Genomics) and 62.5 µL RNase Inhibitor Using serological pipette remove sample (will be in pieces) from the dissociation chamber and place in 5 mL Protein LoBind tube: Centrifuge 1000 x g, 4 °C, 3 minutes Remove supernatant and resuspend in 500 µL of NIR and add 62.5 µL RNase Inhibitor Add tissue in NIR to cold NIC+ cartridge Protector RNase inhibitor Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #3335402001 Rnase Inhibitor can be added based on tissue RNase activity. Lowest suggested final concentration is 0.2 U/µL (12.5 µL) and up to 1 U/µL (62.5 µL) as maximum suggested final concentration.
While Singulator 200 is running prepare 1 mL of nuclei storage reagent (NSR; S2 Genomics) with 1 U/µL RNase inhibitor by adding 25 µL to 1 mL of NSR.
FIXATION AND PERMEABILIZATION Fix using 10x Genomics GEM-X Flex Sample Preparation v2 Kit (PN-1000781) based on 10x Genomics demonstrated protocol (CG000782 rev. D) .
Place cartridge in Singulator 200 and press start. Protocol takes 00:18:00 .
Upon completion of the run, remove cartridge and transfer sample (About 2 mL) into 5 mL Protein LoBind tube.
Centrifuge 500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .
Remove supernatant and resuspend in 1 mL NSR with RNase inhibitor.
Filter nuclei suspension with 30 µm filter to new 1.5 mL Protein LoBind tube. Pre-Separation Filters (30 µm) (Sample Filter, Miltenyi Biotec, 130-041-407)
Centrifuge 500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .
Incubate for one hour at Room temperature or 16-24h at 4 °C . Overnight fixation is recommended if samples will be stored long term (Up to 12 months).
Resuspend nuclei in 500 µL room temperature Fixation Buffer B. Pipette mix 5x.
Remove the supernatant. Up to 30 µL might be left if working with >= 50,000 nuclei .
Count nuclei with DAPI or AO/PI depending on available counter. Make notes about sample quality if a lot of debris is still present then sorting will be needed later before starting single-cell.
Add 500 µL room temperature Additive C (10x Genomics PN-PN2001332) to the sample and pipette mix 5x.
Centrifuge 850 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes .
Add 1 mL chilled Quenching Buffer B to the sample and pipette mix 5x, keep on On ice .
Remove the supernatant. Up to 30 µL might be left if working with >= 50,000 nuclei .
Long-term Storage at -80°C Add 0.1 volume pre-warmed Enhancer (10x Genomics PN-2000482). Add 50% glycerol for final concentration of 10%. Store at -80 °C for up to 12 months.
Nuclei should be thawed according to 10x Genomics demonstrated protocol (CG000782 rev. D) .
Resuspend nuclei in 500 µL Sorting & Collection buffer. Set aside around 5-10% of sample for negative control.

While sorting prepare buffers (Quantities for 1 sample): 1.5 mL 10x Genomics Quenching Buffer B (Step 2.8) or 1.5 mL S&C Buffer. 1 mL PBS-T. Thaw aliquots 500 µL 162 nM 2-amino-5-methylbenzoic acid in PBS. 20 µL 40 µg/mL Proteinase K in PBS (1:500 Stock dilution).
Centrifuge sample 850 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes . Remove supernatant.

Nuclei can be stained either with DAPI or SYTO9. To stain with DAPI, use 2.5 µL 200x DAPI stock into 500 µL sample. To stain with SYTO9: Prepare intermediate SYTO9 solution by mixing 1 µL of 5 mM stock solution with 1 mL of PBS. Prepare working solution by mixing 100 µL of the intermediate stock to 900 µL of PBS. Centrifuge sample 500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes . Remove supernatant and resuspend in 1 mL SYTO9 working solution. Incubate 00:20:00 at room temperature. Wash once with 500 µL Sorting & Collection buffer.
After decrosslinking wash sample twice: Centrifuge 850 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes , remove supernatant, re-suspend in 500 µL PBS-T.
After washes re-suspend in Quenching Buffer. Centrifuge 850 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes , remove supernatant, re-suspend in 500 µL Quenching Buffer B.
Incubate sample on thermoblock (1.5mL) with heated lid: 80 °C 30 min. 22 °C 10 min.
Resuspend sample in 500 µL decrosslinking buffer. Pipette mix 5x.

After sorting immediately proceed to next steps. Next stopping point is only overnight Probe Hybridization during step 1 "Probe Hybridization" (see one of 10x Genomics GEM-X Flex Gene Expression User Guides).

Filter cells/nuclei through a 35 µm filter into Falcon Test Tube before sorting. Falcon™ Test Tube with Cell Strainer Snap Cap (Corning, cat# 352235, https://www.fishersci.com/shop/products/falcon-tube-cell-strainer-cap-mesh-size-35um/0877123 )

Prepare 500 µL decrosslinking buffer: 463 µL 162 nM 2-amino-5-methylbenzoic acid in PBS for final conc. of 150 nM. 31.25 µL 8M Urea for final conc. of 0.5 M. 6.25 µL 40 µg/mL Proteinase K for final conc. of 0.5 µg/mL.
After sorting wash sample once: Centrifuge 850 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes , remove supernatant, re-suspend in 500 µL S&C Buffer.
Alternatively, PCR tubes and thermal cycler could be used to same effect.

After decrosslinking immediately proceed to 10x Genomics Flex assay. Next stopping point is only overnight Probe Hybridization during step 1 "Probe Hybridization" (see one of 10x Genomics GEM-X Flex Gene Expression User Guides).
Prepare Fixation Buffer B , maintain at room temperature. Conc. Fix & Perm Buffer B (10x Genomics PN-2001301) 1X. Formaldehyde 4%. Top off with Nuclease-free Water to 500 µL.
Prepare Quenching Buffer B , maintain at 4 °C. Conc. Quench Buffer B (10x Genomics PN-2001300) 1X. Top off with Nuclease-free Water to 1000 µL.

Proceed to the next step of nuclei sorting via FACS. Important. Samples should be prepared and stored for long-term and processed later after one freeze-thaw cycle. It is recommended to collect all samples up to this point and sort them at once and proceed to Flex.
creator: Karolis Kumpaitis
